reasons: Karrie punishes durable melee fighters with repeated mark damage.
Aulus struggles if he cannot reach Karrie through peel before she stacks sustained damage.

proof.summary: Aulus wants to scale into durable melee DPS, while Karrie can shred frontliners if she is protected.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has peel and enough attack-speed timing.
Aulus is forced to walk through frontline.

reasons: Valir can keep Aulus away from melee range.
Aulus loses much of his value if he cannot stay close enough to keep attacking.

proof.summary: Aulus wants extended melee access, while Valir can slow, push, and reset spacing before Aulus gets full value.
worksBestWhen: Valir holds peel for Aulus's actual entry.
Team backs away rather than brawling in Aulus's range.
failureCases: Aulus enters after Valir's peel cooldown is used.

reasons: Baxia reduces HP regeneration and shield value, helping limit Aulus's extended-fight sustain.
Baxia can contest Aulus's entry without being an easy burst target.

proof.summary: Aulus wants to stay in fights and keep attacking, while Baxia reduces recovery value and makes extended fights less comfortable.
worksBestWhen: Baxia applies anti-regen early in the fight.
failureCases: Aulus ignores Baxia and reaches backline.
Aulus has enough item lead to overpower Baxia anyway.

reasons: Lunox can threaten durable melee heroes with magic burst and HP-sensitive damage.
She can punish Aulus before he reaches the backline.

proof.summary: Aulus wants to tank through fights and keep swinging, while Lunox can pressure him with magic burst before he gets sustained uptime.
worksBestWhen: Team protects her from Aulus's run-down.
failureCases: Lunox is caught without defensive tools.
Aulus reaches her with team follow-up.
Lunox wastes burst on a low-priority target.

reasons: Franco can suppress Aulus during his engage or late-fight carry window.
Aulus is easier to punish when locked before he can keep attacking.

proof.summary: Aulus wants sustained melee uptime, while Franco can stop one key entry window with suppression and create a burst opportunity.
worksBestWhen: Franco saves Bloody Hunt for Aulus after he commits.
Team has enough damage to finish during lock.
failureCases: Franco suppresses too early without follow-up.
Franco is zoned before reaching Aulus.
